channel.title: bchumanist TikTok
channel.description: OK Boomer, all the latest TikToks from bchumanist

item.title: Replying to @lenfriesen Keeping religion and government separate helps everyone, religious believers and non-religious people. We wouldn't want someone using the levers of the state to promote their religion over others or none. Exploring issues around u
item.description: Replying to @lenfriesen Keeping religion and government separate helps everyone, religious believers and non-religious people. We wouldn't want someone using the levers of the state to promote their religion over others or none. Exploring issues around u

item.title: Replying to @grammaschair Exploring issues around unconstitutional #prayer in #CdnPoli municipalities. The decision by the Supreme Court was very clear: you cannot include prayer in a municipal council meeting in #Canada, the state has a duty of religiou
item.description: Replying to @grammaschair Exploring issues around unconstitutional #prayer in #CdnPoli municipalities. The decision by the Supreme Court was very clear: you cannot include prayer in a municipal council meeting in #Canada, the state has a duty of religiou

item.title: Replying to @shywigglr Thank you! We couldn't do our work fighting for the separation of religion and government in #Canada without our amazing volunteer research team and our supporters 💙 #Secular #CdnPoli
item.description: Replying to @shywigglr Thank you! We couldn't do our work fighting for the separation of religion and government in #Canada without our amazing volunteer research team and our supporters 💙 #Secular #CdnPoli

item.title: Replying to @dalton_2022_ Here's why including #Prayer in a municipal council meeting is a problem 🛑🙏 Exploring issues around unconstitutional #prayer in #CdnPoli municipalities. The decision by the Supreme Court was very clear: you cannot include pray
item.description: Replying to @dalton_2022_ Here's why including #Prayer in a municipal council meeting is a problem 🛑🙏 Exploring issues around unconstitutional #prayer in #CdnPoli municipalities. The decision by the Supreme Court was very clear: you cannot include pray

item.title: Replying to @dalton_2022_ Exploring issues around unconstitutional #prayer in #CdnPoli municipalities. The decision by the Supreme Court was very clear: you cannot include prayer in a municipal council meeting in #Canada, the state has a duty of religio
item.description: Replying to @dalton_2022_ Exploring issues around unconstitutional #prayer in #CdnPoli municipalities. The  decision by the Supreme Court was very clear: you cannot include prayer in a municipal council meeting in #Canada, the state has a duty of religio

item.title: Inclusive city council meetings are very demure.
item.description: Inclusive city council meetings are very demure.

item.title: Rossland, BC voted to tax the Catholic Church (at least part of its property).
item.description: Rossland, BC voted to tax the Catholic Church (at least part of its property).

item.title: we get mail
item.description: we get mail

item.title: Check out our BC Election Toolkit at the link in our bio.
item.description: Check out our BC Election Toolkit at the link in our bio.

item.title: Politicians should steer far clear of events that clearly offend the duty of religious neutrality.
item.description: Politicians should steer far clear of events that clearly offend the duty of religious neutrality.

item.title: The latest Ipsos poll from DWDC shows BCers want access to MAID at every publicly funded healthcare facility in the province.
item.description: The latest Ipsos poll from DWDC shows BCers want access to MAID at every publicly funded healthcare facility in the province.

item.title: Replying to @historyin3mins We need to flip the religious freedom argument around and consider first and foremost the rights of individual patients.
item.description: Replying to @historyin3mins We need to flip the religious freedom argument around and consider first and foremost the rights of individual patients.

item.title: Replying to @bicounty Exactly this. At least in Canadian law, people have religious freedom, organizations don't.
item.description: Replying to @bicounty Exactly this. At least in Canadian law, people have religious freedom, organizations don't.

item.title: Our latest report in The Saguenay Project looks at prayers in Saskatchewan municipal council meetings. We continue to find examples of communities violating the 2015 Supreme Court of Canada ruling in MLQ v Saguenay.
item.description: Our latest report in The Saguenay Project looks at prayers in Saskatchewan municipal council meetings. We continue to find examples of communities violating the 2015 Supreme Court of Canada ruling in MLQ v Saguenay.

item.title: The Mayor of Prince Albert defended his city's opening prayer against our report that singled it out as a particularly egregious violation of the state's duty of religious neutrality. We think he's wrong.
item.description: The Mayor of Prince Albert defended his city's opening prayer against our report that singled it out as a particularly egregious violation of the state's duty of religious neutrality. We think he's wrong.

item.title: Replying to @amethyst_geode Territorial acknowledgments are not prayers.
item.description: Replying to @amethyst_geode Territorial acknowledgments are not prayers.

item.title: We were at the BC Court of Appeal yesterday to defend the privacy rights of the ex-religious. #exjw #exjehovahswitness
item.description: We were at the BC Court of Appeal yesterday to defend the privacy rights of the ex-religious. #exjw #exjehovahswitness

item.title: Get ready to fight against White Christian Nationalism. Join us as we work to prevent #Project2025 inspired campaigns coming to Canada. We're taking inspiration from the commitments of our friends and allies at @americanhumanist, @American Atheists, @Sec
item.description: Get ready to fight against White Christian Nationalism. Join us as we work to prevent #Project2025 inspired campaigns coming to Canada. We're taking inspiration from the commitments of our friends and allies at @americanhumanist, @American Atheists, @Sec

item.title: Last month we spoke to The 306 on CBC Saskatchewan about our report on prayers in municipal council meetings.
item.description: Last month we spoke to The 306 on CBC Saskatchewan about our report on prayers in municipal council meetings.

item.title: Toronto City Council recently voted to recognize "Christian Heritage Month." Here is Councillor Gord Perks speaking against the motion on November 14, 2024.
item.description: Toronto City Council recently voted to recognize "Christian Heritage Month." Here is Councillor Gord Perks speaking against the motion on November 14, 2024.

item.title: "Religion divides us" says Toronto City Councillor as he votes against a motion to proclaim "Christian Heritage Month" in the city at the November 14, 2024 council meeting. (Video edited for brevity)
item.description: "Religion divides us" says Toronto City Councillor as he votes against a motion to proclaim "Christian Heritage Month" in the city at the November 14, 2024 council meeting. (Video edited for brevity)

item.title: Recently, Saanich council considered a new permissive tax exemption bylaw. Councillor @tealepb proposed several amendments to provide greater transparency and accountability among recipients (particularly places of worship). While his amendments were defe
item.description: Recently, Saanich council considered a new permissive tax exemption bylaw. Councillor @tealepb proposed several amendments to provide greater transparency and accountability among recipients (particularly places of worship). While his amendments were defe

item.title: City's shouldn't have nativity scenes on public property but they really shouldn't have "keep Christ in Christmas" signs in there. Good work by the Kelowna Atheists, Skeptics and Humanists Association here.
item.description: City's shouldn't have nativity scenes on public property but they really shouldn't have "keep Christ in Christmas" signs in there. Good work by the Kelowna Atheists, Skeptics and Humanists Association here.

item.title: Is the end in sight for religious charities in Canada? MPs on the finance committee agreed it's time to revisit their privileged status.
item.description: Is the end in sight for religious charities in Canada? MPs on the finance committee agreed it's time to revisit their privileged status.

item.title: #stitch with @BC Humanist Association We get fan mail.
item.description: #stitch with @BC Humanist Association We get fan mail.

item.title: Replying to @geigerclick We're funded by about 400 individual small donors. We operate on under $100,000 annually. We also respect uphold our donor's right to privacy.
item.description: Replying to @geigerclick We're funded by about 400 individual small donors. We operate on under $100,000 annually. We also respect uphold our donor's right to privacy.

item.title: #stitch with @CBC Vancouver Canada - and BC in particular - is increasingly diverse and secular. Politicians, especially the ostensibly social democratic premier of the majority non-religious province, should not invoke god in their public speeches. The s
item.description: #stitch with @CBC Vancouver Canada - and BC in particular - is increasingly diverse and secular. Politicians, especially the ostensibly social democratic premier of the majority non-religious province, should not invoke god in their public speeches. The s

item.title: On January 31, atheist Bob LeDrew asked Cobourg council to proclaim Atheist Awareness Day. While they refused his request, they also rescinded a Christian Heritage Month proclamation. Good work Bob! For our part, we're asking city councils to proclaim Apr
item.description: On January 31, atheist Bob LeDrew asked Cobourg council to proclaim Atheist Awareness Day. While they refused his request, they also rescinded a Christian Heritage Month proclamation. Good work Bob! For our part, we're asking city councils to proclaim Apr

item.title: Replying to @sisterearthangel Let's get every municipality that's proclaimed Christian Heritage Month to recognize April 15 as Religious Neutrality Day.
item.description: Replying to @sisterearthangel Let's get every municipality that's proclaimed Christian Heritage Month to recognize April 15 as Religious Neutrality Day.

item.title: Did you know Humanist weddings are illegal in British Columbia? Help us change the law.
item.description: Did you know Humanist weddings are illegal in British Columbia? Help us change the law.

item.title: Replying to @geigerclick Civil marriages must include two specific lines that are not required of religious marriages in BC. The sample ceremony can be modified but is still prescribed by the government for marriage commissioners.
item.description: Replying to @geigerclick Civil marriages must include two specific lines that are not required of religious marriages in BC. The sample ceremony can be modified but is still prescribed by the government for marriage commissioners.

item.title: Happy International Women's Day. We need to continue to resist fascism, Christian Nationalism and tech doomerism that all seek to re-establish the patriarchy and roll back our hard-won rights. #IWD2025 #internationalwomensday
item.description: Happy International Women's Day. We need to continue to resist fascism, Christian Nationalism and tech doomerism that all seek to re-establish the patriarchy and roll back our hard-won rights. #IWD2025 #internationalwomensday

item.title: The BC Court of Appeal upheld the privacy rights of the #exreligious #exjw We intervened in the case and were very pleased with the outcome. But it's also likely not the last battle in this ongoing litigation. Help us continue to represent the voice of t
item.description: The BC Court of Appeal upheld the privacy rights of the #exreligious #exjw We intervened in the case and were very pleased with the outcome. But it's also likely not the last battle in this ongoing litigation. Help us continue to represent the voice of t

item.title: Replying to @egm1991 The JWs could be looking at $100k fines for breaching the Personal Information Protection Act.
item.description: Replying to @egm1991 The JWs could be looking at $100k fines for breaching the Personal Information Protection Act.

item.title: We're going back to court to challenge the exemptions granted to publicly-funded religious service providers.
item.description: We're going back to court to challenge the exemptions granted to publicly-funded religious service providers.

item.title: So what is #humanism
item.description: So what is #humanism

item.title: BC municipalities are officially prayer free following our advocacy. Parksville and Vancouver have committed to keeping future inaugural meetings secular and we've dropped our threats of lawsuits against them. There's still lots to do to defend secularism
item.description: BC municipalities are officially prayer free following our advocacy. Parksville and Vancouver have committed to keeping future inaugural meetings secular and we've dropped our threats of lawsuits against them. There's still lots to do to defend secularism
